topic: Range of Cost of Various Articles

subUnits.title: 8.1 Estimating Prices of Everyday Items
notes: Prices of goods vary by location, shop type, season, and inflation.
Learn to round prices before adding to create quick budgets.
Always add 10-20% buffer for unexpected costs or price fluctuations.

quiz.explanation: 1,450 → 1,500 | 2,680 → 2,700 | 1,500+2,700 = 4,200. (Note: 1450→1500, 2680→2700 = 4200. Let's adjust options to match exact rounding: ₦4,200 is best. I'll fix opts/ans to match exact rounding logic.)
Estimation helps plan spending and prevents going over budget.

subUnits.title: 8.2 Comparing Estimated vs Actual Prices
notes: After estimating, compare with actual market prices to check accuracy.
Price differences occur due to wholesale vs retail, transport costs, or shop location.
Calculate difference: Actual - Estimate = Under/Over estimation amount.
Develops financial literacy, smart purchasing habits, and realistic budgeting skills.

quiz.explanation: 10% difference is generally acceptable for quick estimation.

quiz.q: Which factor affects price range of same item?
quiz.explanation: Prices vary by market location, retail vs wholesale, and demand periods.

quiz.q: If estimated total is ₦10,000 but actual is ₦12,000, what happened?
quiz.explanation: Actual > Estimate means you underestimated the cost.
